NoteText:
HISTORY AND PHYSICAL - GYN ONCOLOGY CONSULT

Patient: 55-year-old postmenopausal female (G3P3) referred for newly diagnosed endometrial cancer.

Chief Complaint: Postmenopausal bleeding x 3 months.

History of Present Illness:
Patient presented to her PCP with intermittent postmenopausal bleeding starting June 2025. She underwent transvaginal ultrasound on 2025-07-20 showing thickened endometrial stripe at 14mm. Office endometrial biopsy performed 2025-08-01 revealed Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma of the endometrium. Referred to GYN oncology for surgical management.

The bleeding has been intermittent, moderate in amount. No associated pain, weight loss, or urinary/bowel symptoms. Last menstrual period was at age 51. No history of hormone replacement therapy.

Past Medical History:
- Hypertension, controlled on lisinopril
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus, on metformin
- Obesity (BMI 38)
- Hyperlipidemia
- No prior surgeries

Family History:
- Mother: colon cancer at age 52
- Maternal aunt: endometrial cancer at age 48
- Brother: colon cancer at age 45
- Pattern concerning for Lynch syndrome

Social History:
- Non-smoker, rare alcohol use
- Retired school teacher
- Lives with spouse
- ECOG performance status: 0

Physical Examination:
- VS: BP 138/82, HR 76, BMI 38
- General: Well-nourished, obese female in no distress
- Abdomen: Soft, obese, no masses or tenderness
- Pelvic: External genitalia normal. Speculum exam: small amount of blood in vault. Cervix appears normal. Bimanual: uterus slightly enlarged, mobile, no adnexal masses.

Assessment:
55-year-old with Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma of the endometrium. Family history concerning for Lynch syndrome. Recommend preoperative workup including MMR IHC, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis for staging, and genetic counseling referral.

Plan:
1. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis for staging
2. Labs: CBC, CMP, CA-125, HE4
3. MMR IHC on biopsy specimen
4. Genetic counseling referral for Lynch syndrome evaluation
5. Preoperative medical clearance given comorbidities
6. Schedule for robotic-assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, sentinel lymph node mapping, and possible pelvic/para-aortic lymphadenectomy

PATHOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE

Reviewed pathology from office endometrial biopsy (2025-08-01):
- Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma
- IHC performed at outside facility: ER 40% positive, PR 10% positive

MMR IHC ordered and resulted today:
- MLH1: LOSS of expression
- PMS2: LOSS of expression
- MSH2: Intact expression
- MSH6: Intact expression

This pattern (MLH1/PMS2 loss) can be due to:
1. Lynch syndrome (germline MLH1 mutation)
2. MLH1 promoter hypermethylation (sporadic)

Given strong family history (mother and brother with colon cancer, aunt with endometrial cancer), Lynch syndrome is highly suspected.

Recommend:
- MLH1 promoter methylation testing
- Germline genetic testing for Lynch syndrome (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM)
- If Lynch negative, consider somatic testing panel

Molecular classification per 2023 FIGO:
- POLE mutation testing pending
- MMR: deficient (MLH1/PMS2 loss)
- Preliminary classification: MMRd (pending POLE result)
- If POLE mutated → POLEmut (supersedes MMRd)
- p53 IHC: wild-type pattern (normal staining)

GENETIC COUNSELING NOTE

Patient seen for genetic counseling regarding concern for Lynch syndrome.

Family History Assessment:
- Amsterdam II criteria: MET (3 relatives with Lynch-associated cancers, 2 successive generations, 1 diagnosed <50)
- Bethesda guidelines: MET (endometrial cancer <60 with MMR deficiency)

Tumor Testing Results:
- MLH1/PMS2 loss by IHC
- MLH1 promoter methylation: NEGATIVE (not hypermethylated)
- This strongly supports germline MLH1 mutation as cause

Germline Testing:
- Comprehensive Lynch syndrome panel sent (Invitae)
- Results (2025-10-15): PATHOGENIC VARIANT detected in MLH1 gene
  - MLH1 c.350C>T (p.Thr117Met) - Known pathogenic variant
  - Classification: Pathogenic

Diagnosis: Lynch Syndrome (MLH1 pathogenic variant)

Implications for cancer management:
- Endometrial cancer is Lynch-associated
- dMMR/MSI-H status → eligible for immunotherapy if advanced/recurrent
- Increased lifetime risk: colon (50-80%), ovarian (6-13%), gastric, urinary tract
- Recommend colonoscopy every 1-2 years
- BSO being performed with hysterectomy (risk-reducing)

Family cascade testing recommended for first-degree relatives.

PREOPERATIVE NOTE

Patient cleared for surgery. Preoperative staging workup complete.

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis (2025-10-05): See radiology report. No evidence of distant metastatic disease. Enlarged uterus with enhancing endometrial mass.

Labs:
- CA-125: 45 U/mL (mildly elevated)
- HE4: 89 pmol/L (normal)
- CBC: WNL
- CMP: Glucose 142, otherwise WNL
- HbA1c: 7.2%

Staging summary:
- Clinical stage: At least IA (Grade 3 endometrioid, depth of invasion unknown until surgical specimen)
- MMRd (MLH1/PMS2 loss, germline MLH1 pathogenic variant - Lynch syndrome)
- POLE mutation: NOT detected (wild-type)
- p53: wild-type pattern

Final 2023 FIGO molecular classification: MMRd
- This affects staging: MMRd status may modify stage group per 2023 FIGO

Surgical plan:
- Robotic-assisted TLH/BSO
- Sentinel lymph node mapping (ICG)
- Pelvic and para-aortic lymph node assessment
- Peritoneal washings

OPERATIVE NOTE

Procedure: Robotic-assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, bilateral sentinel lymph node mapping and biopsy, pelvic washings.

Surgeon: Dr. ZZPROV-Synth, GYN Oncology
Anesthesia: General endotracheal

Findings:
- Uterus enlarged to approximately 10-week size
- Bilateral ovaries and fallopian tubes grossly normal
- No evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis
- No omental disease
- No ascites
- Liver and diaphragm surfaces smooth
- Bilateral sentinel lymph nodes identified (2 left pelvic, 1 right pelvic) - sent for ultrastaging

Procedure Details:
- ICG injected cervically for sentinel lymph node mapping
- Bilateral pelvic sentinel nodes identified and removed
- Standard robotic hysterectomy technique
- Uterus, tubes, and ovaries removed intact via vaginal extraction in specimen bag
- Peritoneal washings obtained
- Vaginal cuff closed in standard fashion
- All ports closed

EBL: 75 mL
Specimens:
1. Uterus, cervix, bilateral tubes and ovaries
2. Left pelvic sentinel lymph nodes (2)
3. Right pelvic sentinel lymph node (1)
4. Peritoneal washings

Complications: None
Patient tolerated procedure well, transferred to recovery in stable condition.

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY - FINAL REPORT

See pathology_reports.csv for full synoptic report.

Summary discussed at GYN tumor board:
- Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma
- Myometrial invasion: 60% (>50% - deep invasion)
- LVSI: Present (extensive)
- Cervical stromal invasion: Absent
- All 3 sentinel lymph nodes: NEGATIVE (including ultrastaging with cytokeratin IHC)
- Peritoneal washings: Negative for malignancy
- Both ovaries and tubes: No tumor

FIGO 2023 Stage: IB (deep myometrial invasion, no cervical/nodal/distant disease)
With molecular classification: Stage IBm-MMRd

This is a high-intermediate risk case:
- Grade 3
- Deep myometrial invasion (>50%)
- Extensive LVSI
- MMR deficient (Lynch syndrome)

Treatment discussion needed: adjuvant therapy decision.

MULTIDISCIPLINARY TUMOR BOARD NOTE

Case presented: 55yo with FIGO Stage IBm-MMRd endometrioid adenocarcinoma.

Key features discussed:
1. High-intermediate risk features: Grade 3, deep invasion, extensive LVSI
2. MMR deficient (Lynch syndrome, germline MLH1 pathogenic variant)
3. 2023 FIGO molecular-integrated staging: IBm-MMRd
4. All sentinel lymph nodes negative

Treatment Discussion:
- Standard of care for high-intermediate risk: Vaginal cuff brachytherapy (VCB) +/- external beam radiation (EBRT)
- MMRd status consideration: GOG-258 and PORTEC-3 data suggest chemotherapy may benefit high-risk patients
- Immunotherapy consideration: dostarlimab (RUBY trial data) approved for dMMR advanced/recurrent endometrial cancer, but not yet standard in adjuvant setting
- NRG-GY018 / KEYNOTE-868: pembrolizumab with chemotherapy for advanced endometrial → supports chemo + IO in appropriate setting

Board Consensus:
1. Adjuvant vaginal cuff brachytherapy (3 fractions)
2. Consider adjuvant chemotherapy (carboplatin/paclitaxel x 4 cycles) given high-intermediate risk features
3. Discuss clinical trial eligibility for adjuvant immunotherapy trials
4. Continue Lynch syndrome surveillance (colonoscopy, upper GI screening)
5. Close follow-up given elevated recurrence risk

Follow-up plan:
- Every 3 months x 2 years with exam and CA-125
- CT imaging if symptoms arise
- Colonoscopy per Lynch syndrome protocol
